print.send_by_post: You now need to send {causa} by registered mail.
print.deadline_info: From the date of receipt, 30 days are allowed to respond. Save a calendar entry to remind yourself to follow up in case you haven't received a response by then.
print.support_us_intro: Our generator was developed by IT and legal experts of the Digital Society in countless hours and is available to everyone for free.
print.support_us_membership: As a <membership_link>member, donor or supporter</membership_link>, you support our work.
print.support_us_newsletter: To stay up to date on our activities, subscribe to our <newsletter_link>monthly newsletter</newsletter_link> now.
print.no_print_text: Unfortunately, printing is not possible on your device. Use the option to send the web address (URL), for example by e-mail, and then open it on a device with a printer. The data you entered is stored in the URL.

app.title: Generate your data access request
app.intro: Under data protection law, every person has the right to find out what data is stored about them, and to have it deleted or corrected if necessary. This right of access allows you to maintain control over your personal data. However, each person must take action themselves and exercise this right.

credits.title: Online Generator for Data Access Requests
credits.website_service: The online generator for data access requests is provided by <digiges_homepage>{digiges_name}</digiges_homepage>.

no_personal_data_collected: No personal data is collected when using the generator: all data entries and selections remain in the user's browser.
url_storage_info: Data entries can be saved by copying the web address (URL). All information is stored in the so-called <fragment>{fragment_label}</fragment> of the URL. This is never sent to a web server. However, the copied URL contains all entered data, which can therefore be reused. The URL should therefore be treated with caution.
notes_for_orgs_p1: Data access requests must be answered within 30 days of receipt. If the deadline is insufficient, the requester must be informed and the deadline by which the information will be provided must be communicated. If the information cannot be provided or cannot be provided in full, this decision must be justified
notes_for_orgs_p2: Compiling the data may require considerable effort. Nevertheless, the information is generally to be provided free of charge. If the purpose or content of the request is unclear, it may be worthwhile – together with the requesting person – to narrow the scope of the request.
further_steps_p1: If no response is received or it is incomplete, a second letter can be sent as a follow-up. Subsequently, legal action to enforce the right of access can be filed against private holders of data collections at the domicile of the data subject or the holder of the data collection
further_steps_p2: It is also possible to request the correction of incorrect data or the deletion of data (and any further collection), provided that this data processing is not justified by an overriding private or public interest or by law
thanks_support_title: Thank you for your financial support
mercator_grant: The project is supported by the <program>{mercator_program}</program> of <mercator>{mercator_org}</mercator>.
danke_freunde_ideen: Thanks to many friends for the ideas ♥️
code_license_info: The <repo>{generator_source_code}</repo> is licensed under the <license>3-Clause BSD Licence</license> and available on GitHub.

ics.description: {days} days ago you generated {causa}. Have you received a response?
ics.followup: Here you can create follow-up letters or additional data access requests
ics.comeback: For questions, please contact us at auskunftsbegehren@digitale-gesellschaft.ch.
